title: Air-fryer tuna steak
subtitle: Seared exterior with a pink or cooked-through centre
excerpt: Fresh tuna steaks brushed with oil and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C. Three minutes per side produces a seared exterior with a pink centre; five minutes per side gives a cooked-through result. The air fryer handles tuna well because the intense heat mimics a very hot pan without requiring any oil in the cooking vessel.
sourceNotes: Fresh tuna steaks became widely available in British supermarkets in the 1990s when yellowfin tuna became a popular alternative to red meat. Unlike tinned tuna, fresh steaks are best served with a pink centre (similar to beef steak) when bought from a reputable source. The air fryer produces results comparable to a cast-iron griddle without the smoke.

content.text: Remove the tuna from the fridge 15 minutes before cooking. A steak that goes into the air fryer cold will overcook on the outside before the centre has warmed through, producing a fully cooked, dry result even at a short cook time. Room-temperature tuna at 200°C achieves the seared exterior and warm pink centre in exactly 6 minutes total.

items.name: Sesame seeds
items.prepNote: to press onto the surface before cooking

content.text: Remove the {{tuna-steak}} from the fridge 15 minutes before cooking. Brush both sides with {{olive-oil}} and season with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Press {{sesame-seeds}} onto both faces if using. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Cook at 200°C for 3 minutes on the first side, then flip carefully and cook for a further 3 minutes for a pink centre (internal temperature 48 to 52°C) or 5 minutes per side for a cooked-through result. Rest for 2 minutes before serving with {{lemon}}.

items.fix: Steaks under 2 cm cook through in 4 to 5 minutes total at 200°C. Always bring to room temperature first. A 2.5 cm steak cooked from room temperature takes 6 minutes for a pink centre
items.cause: Steak too thin, or cooked from cold
items.symptom: Tuna dry and crumbly throughout after 6 minutes

items.fix: Pat dry with kitchen paper before brushing with oil. A thoroughly preheated air fryer at 200°C will colour the surface of a dried, oiled steak within 3 minutes
items.cause: Steak surface not patted dry or not preheated properly
items.symptom: No visible colour on the surface despite correct time

items.fix: Apply sesame seeds only to the surface facing up during the first half of cooking; add them to the second side after flipping. Seeds on the basket-facing side burn against the hot metal
items.symptom: Sesame seeds burning before tuna is cooked

content.text: Air-fryer tuna steak is a fast high-protein dinner. Serve with a dressed salad of rocket, avocado, and capers, over noodles with a soy and ginger dressing, or alongside steamed rice and edamame. The 6-minute cook time is comparable to a griddle pan and produces far less smoke in the kitchen.
